title: Number of Pairs of Interchangeable Rectangles
description:
You are given n rectangles represented by a 0-indexed 2D integer array rectangles, where rectangles[i] = [widthi, heighti] denotes the width and height of the ith rectangle.

Two rectangles i and j (i < j) are considered interchangeable if they have the same width-to-height ratio. More formally, two rectangles are interchangeable if widthi/heighti == widthj/heightj (using decimal division, not integer division).

Return _the number of pairs of interchangeable rectangles in_ rectangles.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def canReach(s: str, minJump: int, maxJump: int) -> bool:
    n = len(s)
    dp = [False] * n
    dp[0] = True
    pre = 0
    for i in range(1, n):
        if i >= minJump:
            pre += dp[i - minJump]
        if i > maxJump:
            pre -= dp[i - maxJump - 1]
        dp[i] = pre > 0 and s[i] == '0'
    return dp[n - 1]
```

    
    The algorithm uses dynamic programming to determine whether it is possible to reach the end of the string. The `dp` array keeps track of the positions that can be reached. At each position, it checks whether it is possible to reach this position using valid jumps. Then, it calculates the prefix sum `pre` to keep track of the number of ways to reach the current position using valid jumps. If it is reachable, it sets the `dp` value of that position to true. Finally, it returns the `dp` value of `s.length - 1`. If it is true, you can reach the end of the string, otherwise, you cannot.
